fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Minnesota Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Minnesota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity minnesota, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to minnesota, speech-language pathologist compact minnesota, minnesota speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Speech-Language Pathologist, Audiologist, and Hearing Instrument Dispenser Advisory Council
board.mailing_address: Minnesota Department of Health, Health Regulation Division, P.O. Box 64970, St. Paul, MN 55164-0970
board.physical_address: 85 E. 7th Place, Suite 220, St. Paul, MN 55164

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Minnesota.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP Registration
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Create an Account and Start Application
steps.description: Register for a new account on the MDH eLicense portal. Once logged in, select the application for Speech-Language Pathologist licensure.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Fill out the entire application form within the MDH eLicense portal, providing all requested personal, educational, and professional history.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload or arrange for official submission of all required documents, including official transcripts from your graduate program, official Praxis exam scores, and a completed Clinical Fellowship Report (if applying by examination). For endorsement, ensure current license verification.

steps.title: Arrange for License Verification (Endorsement)
steps.description: If you hold an active license in another state, arrange for each issuing licensing board to send official verification of your license directly to the Minnesota Department of Health.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $100 through the MDH eLicense portal to finalize your application submission.

steps.title: Await Review and Approval
steps.description: The Minnesota Department of Health will review your complete application and all submitted documentation. You will be notified of your licensure status once the review is complete.

telepractice_text: Minnesota permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Minnesota offers a Provisional SLP Registration for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Minnesota is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a Minnesota SLP license cost?
Is Minnesota part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Minnesota allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Minnesota have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Minnesota. To work in Minnesota, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Minnesota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Minnesota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Minnesota is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Minnesota
hero.dek: To apply for Speech-Language Pathologist licensure in Minnesota, you must submit your application and all supporting documentation through the MDH eLicense portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the MDH eLicense portal and click 'Register' to create a new account using your email address and preferred password. Follow the prompts to verify your email and complete the initial profile setup before starting an application.
